Reviews.Author: 1y05jymm4n
Reviews.Comment:
Real is an absolute powerhouse under the hood. It competes directly with top tier executors on a technical level; perfect function support, elite stability, and security that holds up.

However, the "user-facing" side is a mess. The fact that it forces itself into your %localappdata% regardless of your choice.

Categories.Comment:
Real delivers perfect compatibility:
- 100% sUNC
-  All functions supported (56/56)
- Handles complex scripts with ease

Experience: Zero missing functions. Every tested sUNC function returned present; no gaps, no workarounds needed. Scripts that rely on deep executor functionality just work.

Average: ~4.8M iterations/sec

- Stable across all 6 stress stages
- No speed degradation under extreme load
- Efficient resource usage throughout

Experience: Remarkably consistent. Most executors start bleeding performance around the Heavy or Very Heavy stage.

Big Integer Benchmark F(10,000):

Time: 4.1455 seconds
Solid result. Not the fastest I've seen for Fibonacci benchmarks, but well within the acceptable range for real-world usage.

One real vulnerability out of 113 tested is a strong result. The skipped tests are largely OS-level functions the executor doesn't support, that's not a flaw, that's scope. The Robux API exposure is worth monitoring, but it's a single vector in an otherwise well-secured environment.

Even if you manually choose a specific installation folder, Real ignores your preference for core files.

- AppData Bloat: It forces its way into %localappdata% regardless of where you put the executable.
The developers are geniuses at coding an execution engine & coding an UI.
